message:
fix(cli): validate reservation ID is positive in force-release command

Add bounds checking for the reservation ID argument in the
'ntm locks force-release' command. Previously, only non-numeric
input was rejected via strconv.Atoi, but negative numbers and
zero would be accepted and cause confusing behavior downstream.

The fix adds explicit validation that the ID must be >= 1:
- Zero is rejected: IDs are 1-indexed in the database
- Negative numbers are rejected: these are never valid reservation IDs

Error message updated to be more specific:
- Before: "must be a number"
- After: "must be a positive number" (for <= 0 cases)

This prevents users from accidentally passing invalid IDs that would
either fail silently or produce confusing "reservation not found"
errors when the real issue is input validation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
